title: hieracium ramondii subsp. neoleontodon
intro: Hieracium ramondii subsp. neoleontodon, also known as the hawkweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and acting as an antiseptic. Hieracium ramondii subsp. neoleontodon can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its medicinal uses.
